text: Medial tibial stress syndrome (MTSS) affects 5%-35% of runners
Previous history of MTSS is the strongest risk factor for developing MTSS, with a risk ratio of 3.74
Prior use of orthotics is significantly associated with increased MTSS risk, with a risk ratio of 2.31
Female runners have a significantly higher risk of developing MTSS compared to males, with a risk ratio of 1.71
Fewer years of running experience is significantly associated with increased MTSS risk
Increased body mass index is a significant risk factor for MTSS development
Navicular drop greater than 10 mm is associated with approximately double the risk of developing MTSS
specific_value: RR 1.99 (95% CI 1.00-3.96, P=0.05) for navicular drop >10 mm

text: Increased hip external rotation range of motion in males is significantly associated with MTSS risk
